metadata.description: Care Partner KB: Condition definitions, lab monitoring, treatment protocols. Plain English for member education.
metadata.disclaimer: Educational information only. Never constitutes a diagnosis. Member should discuss with their physician.

entries.plain_english: Your body has trouble using insulin to control blood sugar levels. Over time, high blood sugar damages blood vessels and nerves throughout the body.

treatment_options.details: Medical Nutrition Therapy + 150 min/week moderate aerobic exercise reduces HbA1c by 0.5–1.0% and can achieve remission in some patients

treatment_options.source: ADA 2024 + FDA labeling + LEADER trial NEJM 2016

HbA1c.frequency: Every 3 months until at goal, then every 6 months

lipid_panel.target_LDL: <70 mg/dL for high CV risk diabetics

dilated_eye_exam.frequency: Annually (every 2 years if no retinopathy and good control)
dilated_eye_exam.reason: Diabetic retinopathy — leading cause of blindness in working-age adults

foot_exam.reason: Diabetic neuropathy and peripheral vascular disease

preventive_coverage.medicare: Diabetes Prevention Program covered (CDC-recognized). Annual foot exam covered. Eye exam annually if diabetic retinopathy risk.
preventive_coverage.uhc: DPP program covered for eligible members. CGM coverage with prior auth.

medication_coverage.medicare_part_d: Metformin generic covered on most formularies Tier 1. GLP-1/SGLT-2 may be Tier 3-4 with prior auth. Insulin covered under Part D or Part B (if in pump).
medication_coverage.uhc: Formulary varies by plan. Step therapy may require metformin failure before brand coverage.

dme.medicare: Blood glucose monitors covered under Part B. CGM covered for Type 1 and insulin-dependent Type 2 meeting criteria.
dme.uhc: BGM and test strips covered. CGM with PA for qualifying patients.

source.document: ADA Standards of Medical Care in Diabetes 2024

entries.plain_english: Your heart is working too hard to pump blood through your arteries. Over time, this damages blood vessels and increases your risk of heart attack, stroke, and kidney disease.

treatment_options.details: DASH diet reduces BP 8-14 mmHg. Sodium restriction <2300mg/day reduces 2-8 mmHg. Weight loss 1mmHg per kg lost. Exercise 90-150 min/week moderate aerobic.

drug_classes.preferred_for: Diabetes with proteinuria, heart failure, post-MI
Elderly, African Americans, angina, high CV risk

bp_targets.note_conflict: JNC8 (2014) used <150/90 for age 60+; ACC/AHA 2017 recommends <130/80 for most. Current standard follows ACC/AHA 2017. See kb_conflicts.md CONFLICT_001.

preventive_coverage.medicare: Blood pressure screening covered annually under Welcome to Medicare visit and Annual Wellness Visit. No copay for preventive screening.
preventive_coverage.uhc: BP screening covered under preventive benefit. Home BP monitors not typically covered.

medication_coverage.medicare_part_d: Generic antihypertensives (HCTZ, lisinopril, amlodipine, losartan) Tier 1 on most formularies.
medication_coverage.uhc: Generics Tier 1. Brand antihypertensives may require step therapy.

entries.plain_english: Fatty deposits (plaque) have built up inside the arteries that supply your heart muscle with blood. This narrows the arteries and can cause chest pain, heart attack, or sudden cardiac death.

drugs.name: Aspirin
drugs.note: For established CAD. USPSTF recommends against initiating aspirin for primary prevention in adults 60+.

drugs.name: High-intensity statin
drugs.role: LDL reduction 50%+, plaque stabilization, mortality reduction

drugs.name: Beta-blocker
drugs.role: Reduces heart rate and oxygen demand, post-MI mortality benefit

drugs.name: ACE inhibitor or ARB
drugs.role: Heart failure prevention, renal protection, BP control

preventive_coverage.medicare: Annual ECG covered at Welcome to Medicare visit. Cardiac rehab covered Part B after MI/CABG/stable angina (up to 36 sessions).
preventive_coverage.uhc: Cardiac rehab covered post-procedure. Prior auth may be required.

medication_coverage.medicare_part_d: Generic aspirin not covered (OTC). Generic statins Tier 1. Beta-blockers generics Tier 1.
medication_coverage.uhc: Generic formulary Tier 1 for most CAD medications.

source.document: AHA/ACC 2023 Guideline for the Diagnosis and Management of Chronic Coronary Disease

entries.condition_name: Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction (HFrEF)
entries.plain_english: Your heart muscle is weak and not pumping enough blood to meet your body's needs. This causes fluid to back up into your lungs and legs. HFrEF means the heart's pumping chamber (left ventricle) squeezes less than normal (EF <40%).

treatment_options.note: GDMT for HFrEF — four pillars proven to reduce mortality

drugs.source: AHA/ACC 2022 HF Guideline — Class I recommendation

drugs.class: ACE inhibitor or ARB or ARNI
drugs.preferred: Sacubitril/valsartan (Entresto) over ACEi if tolerated (PARADIGM-HF: 20% mortality reduction)

drugs.benefit: 30% mortality reduction (RALES trial) in severe HF
drugs.watch: Hyperkalemia — check K+ within 1 week of initiation

drugs.benefit: 25% reduction in CV death/HF hospitalization (DAPA-HF, EMPEROR-Reduced) — regardless of diabetes status
drugs.source: DAPA-HF NEJM 2019 + AHA/ACC 2022 — Class I, new 2022

monitoring.weight: Daily (alert care team if >2 lbs/day or >5 lbs/week weight gain)
monitoring.echocardiogram: At diagnosis, 3-6 months after GDMT optimization
monitoring.renal_electrolytes: Every 1-2 weeks after medication changes, then quarterly

insurance.medicare: HF disease management program covered. Home health covered if homebound. Cardiac resynchronization therapy (CRT) covered if LVEF ≤35% + LBBB.
insurance.uhc: Case management available for HF patients. ARNI (Entresto) may require prior auth and step therapy.

entries.plain_english: The upper chambers of your heart (atria) beat in a chaotic, irregular rhythm instead of a steady beat. This can cause a fast or irregular pulse, fatigue, and shortness of breath. Most importantly, AFib greatly increases stroke risk because blood can pool and clot in the atria.

stroke_risk_scoring.anticoagulation_threshold: Score ≥2 in males, ≥3 in females — anticoagulation recommended

treatment_options.preferred: DOAC (Direct Oral Anticoagulants) over warfarin for most patients
treatment_options.note: Warfarin still used for mechanical valves and certain CKD stages. DOACs preferred for non-valvular AFib.
treatment_options.source: AHA/ACC/HRS 2023 — Class I recommendation for DOACs over warfarin

insurance.medicare_part_d: DOACs are Tier 3-4 on most formularies — significant copay. Generic warfarin Tier 1.
insurance.uhc: DOACs may require prior auth. Step therapy protocols vary. Exception process available for clinical reasons.

entries.plain_english: COPD is a long-term lung disease where the airways are damaged and narrowed, making it hard to breathe. It includes emphysema (damaged air sacs) and chronic bronchitis (inflamed airways with cough). It's usually caused by long-term smoking.

treatment_options.combination: LAMA + LABA (e.g., Anoro Ellipta, Stiolto) superior to either alone for symptom reduction

treatment_options.note: ICS + LABA + LAMA (triple therapy) for frequent exacerbators or eosinophil count ≥300
treatment_options.caution: ICS increases pneumonia risk — weigh against exacerbation reduction

treatment_options.details: 6-12 week structured exercise program reduces hospitalization by 50%, improves quality of life
Only intervention proven to slow FEV1 decline. Combination NRT + varenicline (Chantix) most effective.

monitoring.exacerbation_history: Track frequency (predicts future exacerbations and hospitalization)
monitoring.oxygen_saturation: Home pulse oximetry if severe COPD
monitoring.vaccinations: Annual flu, COVID, pneumococcal (PCV20 or PCV15+PPSV23)

insurance.medicare: Pulmonary rehabilitation covered under Part B (36 sessions). Home oxygen covered if SpO2 ≤88% at rest (Part B DME). Spirometry covered for diagnosis and monitoring.
insurance.uhc: Pulmonary rehab covered with PA. Smoking cessation counseling covered under preventive benefit.

source.document: GOLD 2024 Global Strategy for Prevention, Diagnosis and Management of COPD

entries.plain_english: Your kidneys are not filtering blood as well as they should. CKD develops slowly over years, often from diabetes or high blood pressure. Stage 3 means your kidneys are working at 30-60% of normal capacity.

staging.stage_1: eGFR ≥90 (normal or high with kidney damage markers)
staging.stage_4: eGFR 15-29 (severely decreased — prepare for renal replacement therapy)

drugs.name: ACE inhibitor or ARB
drugs.role: Reduces proteinuria and slows CKD progression, especially in diabetic nephropathy

drugs.name: SGLT-2 inhibitor (finerenone or dapagliflozin)
drugs.role: Finerenone (Kerendia) reduces CKD progression in diabetic CKD (FIDELIO trial). Dapagliflozin reduces CKD progression (DAPA-CKD)

insurance.medicare: CKD education covered (Part B) for Stages 4-5 (up to 6 sessions). ESRD: Medicare eligible at any age with ESRD (Part B covers dialysis, Part A covers kidney transplant inpatient).
insurance.uhc: Disease management programs for CKD. Dialysis covered. Transplant medication covered under PA.

source.document: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for CKD

entries.plain_english: You have too much cholesterol or other fats (lipids) in your blood. LDL ('bad') cholesterol builds up in arteries and causes heart attacks and strokes. HDL ('good') cholesterol helps remove LDL. High triglycerides also increase heart disease risk.

LDL.target_very_high_risk: <70 mg/dL (established ASCVD or DM with risk factors)

treatment_options.details: Mediterranean diet or DASH: reduces LDL 10-20%. Soluble fiber (oatmeal, psyllium): reduces LDL 5-10%. Plant sterols 2g/day: reduces LDL 10%. Exercise: raises HDL 5-10%.

treatment_options.monitoring: CK and LFTs if symptomatic myopathy
treatment_options.source: ACC/AHA 2019 — Class I for established ASCVD

drugs.name: PCSK9 inhibitors (evolocumab, alirocumab)
drugs.use: Familial hypercholesterolemia or very high risk
drugs.note: Expensive — PA usually required, step therapy through statins + ezetimibe first

insurance.medicare_part_d: Generic statins (atorvastatin, simvastatin, pravastatin) Tier 1. Rosuvastatin generic available. Ezetimibe generic available. PCSK9 inhibitors Tier 5 — significant OOP cost, require PA.
insurance.uhc: Step therapy: statin first, then ezetimibe, then PCSK9 with PA and documented statin intolerance or inadequate response.

source.document: ACC/AHA 2019 Guideline on Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease

entries.plain_english: Depression is a medical condition where you feel persistently sad, empty, or hopeless for at least two weeks. It affects how you think, feel, and handle daily activities. Depression is not a character flaw — it's a brain chemistry and stress response disorder that responds well to treatment.

diagnostic_criteria.dsm5_requires: 5+ symptoms for ≥2 weeks, at least one being depressed mood or loss of interest

approaches: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) — equal efficacy to antidepressants for mild-moderate
Exercise — 150 min/week reduces depressive symptoms (comparable to SSRI in some trials)

agents.name: Fluoxetine (Prozac)
agents.preferred_for: Long half-life (good if adherence concern), adolescents

treatment_options.trial_duration: 6-8 weeks at therapeutic dose before declaring failure
treatment_options.source: APA Practice Guidelines 2023 + FDA approved labeling

crisis_resources.immediate: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline — call or text 988

insurance.mental_health_parity: Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA) requires mental health benefits equal to medical benefits.
insurance.medicare: Mental health outpatient covered under Part B (20% after deductible). Inpatient under Part A. Psychotherapy covered.
insurance.uhc: Behavioral health covered. Virtual therapy (Optum) available. Prior auth may be required for higher levels of care.

source.document: APA Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Patients with Major Depressive Disorder, 3rd ed

entries.plain_english: Asthma is a chronic lung condition where airways become inflamed and narrow, causing wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Triggers (allergens, exercise, cold air) cause temporary airway narrowing. Most people with asthma can live a fully active life with proper treatment.

stepwise_treatment.step_1_intermittent: Albuterol (SABA) as needed only. No daily controller needed.
stepwise_treatment.step_2_mild_persistent: Low-dose inhaled corticosteroid (ICS) daily — e.g., budesonide 180mcg BID or fluticasone 88mcg BID. PRN albuterol.
stepwise_treatment.step_3_moderate_persistent: Low-dose ICS + LABA (e.g., fluticasone/salmeterol or budesonide/formoterol).
stepwise_treatment.step_4_severe_persistent: Medium-dose ICS + LABA. Consider tiotropium add-on.
stepwise_treatment.step_5_very_severe: High-dose ICS + LABA + biologics (dupilumab, mepolizumab, omalizumab based on phenotype).

entries.rescue_inhaler_note: Albuterol (blue inhaler) is rescue only. Reliance >2 days/week indicates poorly controlled asthma — step up controller therapy.

monitoring.spirometry: At diagnosis, after treatment stabilization, annually
monitoring.peak_flow_monitoring: Home peak flow meter for severe/unstable asthma

insurance.medicare: Nebulizer covered Part B if medical necessity. ICS and SABA covered Part D. Biologics (Dupixent) Part D or Part B if infused — PA required.
insurance.uhc: Branded inhalers require PA and step therapy through generics. Biologics require prior auth.

source.document: NAEPP Expert Panel Report 4: Asthma Management Guidelines (2020)

entries.plain_english: Your thyroid gland is underactive and produces too little thyroid hormone. Thyroid hormone controls metabolism — when it's low, everything slows down: heart rate, metabolism, energy, and mood.
causes: Hashimoto's thyroiditis (autoimmune — most common in US)

TSH.frequency: Every 6-12 months when stable; every 6-8 weeks after dose change

free_T4.frequency: With TSH at diagnosis and dose changes

treatment_options.note: Levothyroxine (Synthroid, generic L-T4) is standard of care
treatment_options.starting_dose: 1.6 mcg/kg/day (reduce for elderly and cardiac patients: start 25-50mcg)
treatment_options.administration: Take on empty stomach, 30-60 min before breakfast. Avoid calcium, iron within 4 hours.
treatment_options.side_effects: At correct dose: none. Overdose signs: tachycardia, insomnia, weight loss, anxiety.

insurance.medicare_part_d: Generic levothyroxine: Tier 1 on virtually all formularies. Brand Synthroid: Tier 3.
insurance.note: TSH lab covered under Part B when medically necessary.

source.document: ATA Guidelines for Hypothyroidism in Adults (2014, updated recommendations 2023)

entries.plain_english: Obesity means having a body mass index (BMI) ≥30. It's a complex chronic disease involving genetics, environment, behavior, and metabolism — not simply a willpower issue. Obesity significantly increases risk of diabetes, heart disease, sleep apnea, joint disease, and several cancers.

treatment_options.details: Intensive behavioral therapy: 14+ sessions/year. 5-10% weight loss reduces diabetes risk 58% (DPP trial). Diet quality > calorie counting alone.

agents.name: Tirzepatide (Zepbound) 15mg weekly
agents.effect: 20-22% weight loss (SURMOUNT trials — greater than any prior agent)

treatment_options.source: STEP trials NEJM 2021, SURMOUNT-1 NEJM 2022, Endocrine Society 2015

insurance.medicare: Intensive Behavioral Therapy (IBT) for obesity covered under Part B (no copay when billed as preventive). Bariatric surgery covered if BMI ≥35 with comorbidity (with prior auth). Anti-obesity medications: NOT covered under standard Part D (AOM exclusion — policy gap as of 2024).
insurance.uhc: Coverage of AOMs varies by employer plan. Most commercial plans do not cover Wegovy/Zepbound. Check specific plan formulary.
insurance.note: CMS proposed covering AOMs under Part D in 2024 — policy evolution ongoing.

source.document: Endocrine Society Obesity Guideline 2015 + ASMBS 2022 Bariatric Surgery Guideline

entries.plain_english: Osteoporosis means your bones have become weak and brittle, increasing fracture risk. The spine, hip, and wrist are most commonly fractured. A hip fracture in an older adult is a major health event — 20-30% of patients die within a year.

diagnostic_criteria.treatment_threshold: Hip fracture risk ≥3% or major osteoporotic fracture risk ≥20% on FRAX

treatment_options.details: Calcium 1000-1200mg/day (diet preferred over supplements), Vitamin D 600-800 IU/day (1000-2000 IU if deficient), Weight-bearing exercise 30 min most days, Fall prevention (physical therapy, home safety assessment)

treatment_options.note: Take alendronate with full glass water, remain upright 30 min — risk of esophagitis if not
treatment_options.drug_holiday: Consider 'holiday' after 5 years oral or 3 years IV if stable

insurance.medicare: DXA covered every 2 years for osteoporosis screening (women ≥65, men with risk). More frequently if on treatment. Zoledronic acid covered Part B (physician-administered infusion).
insurance.uhc: DXA screening covered per USPSTF guidelines. Bisphosphonates Part D.

source.document: National Osteoporosis Foundation Clinician's Guide to Prevention and Treatment of Osteoporosis (2020)

entries.plain_english: GERD is when stomach acid frequently flows back up into your esophagus (food pipe), causing heartburn, chest discomfort, and sometimes damage to the esophageal lining.

treatment_options.details: Avoid trigger foods (spicy, fatty, coffee, alcohol, citrus, chocolate, peppermint). Eat smaller meals. No eating within 3 hours of bedtime. Elevate head of bed 6-8 inches. Weight loss. Stop smoking.

agents.name: Proton Pump Inhibitors (PPIs)
agents.note: Most effective — take 30-60 min before first meal. Long-term risks: B12 deficiency, hypomagnesemia, possible increased fracture risk. Use lowest effective dose.

agents.name: H2 blockers
agents.note: Less effective than PPIs, good for breakthrough or mild symptoms

alarm_symptoms.reason: Screen for Barrett's esophagus and esophageal adenocarcinoma

insurance.medicare_part_d: Generic PPIs (omeprazole, pantoprazole) OTC but also covered as generic Tier 1 when prescribed.
insurance.endoscopy_coverage: Diagnostic endoscopy covered Part B with appropriate indication.

entries.plain_english: GAD is excessive, difficult-to-control worry about many different things, most days for at least 6 months. The worry causes real physical symptoms (muscle tension, fatigue, trouble sleeping) and significantly interferes with daily life.

diagnostic_criteria.dsm5: Excessive anxiety ≥6 months about multiple topics, with ≥3 physical symptoms

approaches: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) — highest evidence for GAD

treatment_options.note: Benzodiazepines (Xanax, Klonopin, Ativan) are NOT first-line for GAD — dependence risk, cognitive impairment in elderly. Use only short-term for acute exacerbations with careful monitoring.

insurance.note: Same Mental Health Parity rules as depression — see CONDITION_DEPRESSION_001 insurance section.
insurance.medicare: Outpatient therapy covered Part B. Psychiatry covered Part B.
